title: Mustardy Gooey American Pecorino Gratin
description: Rich and savory individual mini-gratin featuring a mustardy, gooey American pecorino, perfect for the holiday season. Each cocotte is filled to the brim with a crunchy, golden-brown crust and a creamy, cheesy interior.
instructions: Roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out 6-8 squares of puff pastry, depending on the size of your mini cocottes.
In a small bowl, mix together the pecorino, wine, cream, mustard, brown sugar, garlic, and thyme.
Season the mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Place a square of puff pastry in each mini cocotte, pressing it into the corners and up the sides of the dish.
Fill each cocotte with the pecorino mixture, leaving about 1/2 inch of space at the top.
Brush the edges of the puff pastry with the beaten egg and fold the pastry over the filling to form a crust.
Press the edges of the pastry to seal and use a sharp knife to cut a few slits in the top of each cocotte.
Place the cocottes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with the remaining egg.
Bake the gratins for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
Serve the gratins hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
Let the gratins rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the filling to set.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make more gratins.
